payload.text:
design an AI-powered app that uses Google's "Nano Banana" (Gemini's image-generation model) to let users visualize different hairstyles on their own photo. The same approach can later be extended to pets, makeup, fashion, and more.

A web or mobile app that:

Asks questions about the user’s (or pet’s) personality, face shape, hair type, and style preferences.
Uploads a photo of the subject (person or animal).
Generates realistic AI images showing the subject with various hairstyles/grooming styles.
Allows comparison and selection of the best look.
Nano Banana is the name for Gemini's native image-generation capabilities. It’s ideal for personalized images like trying on hairstyles because it can:

Generate and edit images conversationally using text, images, or both[reference:0].
Maintain character consistency – keep the person’s (or pet’s) face identical while changing only the hair[reference:1].
Make local edits – perfect for modifying just the hairstyle in an uploaded photo[reference:2].
Offer two model tiers:
Nano Banana (Fast) – for quick, everyday use.
Nano Banana Pro (Thinking) – for advanced, high-quality outputs with precise control[reference:3].
Tip: Google’s support article explicitly mentions that Nano Banana is “ideal for creating personalised images, such as when you want to view yourself with different hairstyles”[reference:4].

Get a free API key from Google AI Studio.
Choose the model:
For speed: gemini-2.5-flash-image (Nano Banana)
For quality: gemini-3-pro-image-preview (Nano Banana Pro)
Install the client library (e.g., @google/genai for JavaScript, google-genai for Python).
Upload Photo: Let users upload a clear, front-facing photo.
Ask Questions: A short quiz to gather input. Example questions:
What is your face shape? (Oval, round, square, heart, diamond)
What is your hair texture? (Straight, wavy, curly, coily)
How much maintenance are you willing to do? (Low, medium, high)
Describe your personal style (e.g., classic, edgy, romantic, sporty).
(For pets) What is your pet’s breed? Size? Personality?
Generate Prompts: Combine the answers into a detailed text prompt for Gemini.
Use the uploaded photo as a reference image and describe the desired hairstyle. Here are examples from a curated list of Gemini AI hairstyle prompts[reference:5]:

For a sleek bob: “Woman with sleek chin-length bob, side part, smooth straight hair, natural shine, soft indoor lighting, realistic hairstyle, using reference photo”[reference:6].

For a textured crop: “Woman with short layered crop, tousled texture, natural volume, soft highlights, realistic hair strands, casual indoor lighting”[reference:7].

Prompt template for your app: "Create a hyper-realistic image of the person in the uploaded photo with [hairstyle description]. Keep the face, skin, and background identical. Only change the hair. The hairstyle should be [length], [texture], [volume], with [details]. Use natural lighting."

Here’s a Python example using the Gemini API to generate an image from a text prompt and an uploaded photo:

from google import genai
from PIL import Image

client = genai.Client(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")

# Load the user's uploaded photo
user_image = Image.open("path/to/uploaded_photo.jpg")

# Construct the prompt based on the user's quiz answers
prompt = """
Create a hyper-realistic image of the person in the uploaded photo with a chin-length bob, side part, smooth straight hair, and natural shine.
Keep the face, skin, and background identical. Only change the hair.
Use soft indoor lighting.
"""

# Generate the new image
response = client.models.generate_content(
    model="gemini-2.5-flash-image",  # or "gemini-3-pro-image-preview"
    contents=[prompt, user_image]
)

# Save the result
for part in response.parts:
    if part.inline_data is not None:
        generated_image = part.as_image()
        generated_image.save("generated_hairstyle.png")

A modern React-based web app is a great choice. You can use the open-source Hairstyle AI Studio project as a starting point. It’s a React/Vite app that uses Gemini AI for facial analysis and hairstyle rendering[reference:8].

Clone the repository: git clone https://github.com/ryanbaumann/hairstyle-ai-studio.git
Add your API key to the .env file.
Modify the prompt logic to incorporate your quiz answers.
Show the generated images in a grid or side-by-side view. Allow users to:

Compare multiple styles.
Refine results with natural-language feedback (e.g., “make it wavier”).
Download or share their favorites.
The same architecture works for any subject:

Adjust the questions: Ask about the pet’s breed, size, fur type, and personality.
Adapt the prompts: “Create a hyper-realistic image of the dog in the uploaded photo with a tidy poodle clip. Keep the body and background identical. Only style the fur.”
Use the same API call – Gemini handles animal images just as well as human ones.
Once the core hairstyle app is built, you can reuse the same foundation for:

Makeup & Cosmetics: Change eye shadow, lip color, foundation.
Fashion & Outfits: Try on different clothes, accessories, shoes.
Suits & Formal Wear: Visualize tuxedos, gowns, etc.
Costume Design: Experiment with theatrical, fantasy, or holiday looks.
Each expansion simply requires:

A new set of questions (e.g., “What’s your skin tone?” for makeup).
A tailored prompt library (e.g., “Apply a matte red lipstick and winged eyeliner”).
The same Gemini API image-editing call.
Privacy: Clearly inform users that their photos are sent to Google’s servers for processing. Avoid storing sensitive images permanently.
Bias: AI models can reflect societal biases in beauty standards. Consider adding a disclaimer that results are AI-generated suggestions.
Cost: The Gemini API has usage-based pricing. Estimate costs based on the number of images generated.
Terms of Service: Ensure your app complies with Google’s Prohibited Use Policy (e.g., no deceptive or harmful content)[reference:9].
| Resource | Description | |----------|-------------| | Gemini API Image Generation Docs | Official guide for Nano Banana image generation[reference:10]. | | Hairstyle AI Studio (GitHub) | Sample React app using Gemini for hairstyles[reference:11]. | | 30 Best Gemini AI Hairstyle Prompts | Ready-to-use prompt examples[reference:12]. | | Generate and Edit Images with Gemini Apps | Google’s guide on using Nano Banana for personalized images[reference:13]. |

Start by cloning the Hairstyle AI Studio repo, getting a Gemini API key, and modifying the prompt logic to incorporate your quiz. This approach gives you a working foundation quickly. As you iterate, you can add the pet module, then expand into makeup, fashion, and beyond.

Good luck with your app! If you hit specific technical snags (e.g., prompt engineering for pets, API integration details), feel free to ask for more targeted help.

I will now update the application to utilize the **Nano Banana** (Gemini image generation) models. 

### Specification

1.  **AI Integration**: 
    *   I'll replace the text-based recommendation analysis with actual image generation using `gemini-2.5-flash-image`.
    *   The app will use the user's uploaded or captured photo as a reference `inlineData` part alongside a descriptive prompt derived from their "vibe" and quiz answers.
2.  **UI Updates**:
    *   **Results Screen**: Instead of just showing text, the "AI Pick" will now trigger a generation process that produces a new image of the user with the recommended hairstyle.
    *   **Loading State**: I'll implement a "Processing Lab" overlay that informs the user that the "Nano Banana" model is "peeling" their new look.
3.  **Behavior**:
    *   The `geminiService` will now return the base64-encoded generated image.
    *   I'll maintain the "Pet" vs "Human" mode, tailoring the generation prompt accordingly.

entries.description: Update service to use gemini-2.5-flash-image for actual image generation and editing (visualizing hairstyles).
Update ScanScreen to call the new image generation service and handle the specific output.
Update results screen to display the generated AI image instead of a placeholder.
